_instructions: This file holds ALL the editable text on the KhanConcepts website. Each top-level key below is one section of the site — the '_section' note inside it tells you where that section appears. Only edit the plain text values; do not rename or remove any keys. Wrapping a phrase in **double asterisks** (e.g. **like this**) makes it show in gold on the site.

preloader._section: Preloader — the loading screen shown once before the site reveals. 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).

hero._section: Homepage — Hero (the very first section visitors see, with the big background image and logo) — 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).
hero.tagline: Discovering **Real-World Problems** and Creating **Innovative Concept Designs** That Help Companies Create **Better Solutions**

what-is-khanconcepts._section: Homepage — 'What Is KhanConcepts?' (second section, directly below the Hero) — 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).
what-is-khanconcepts.paragraph1: KhanConcepts **researches real-world problems** and creates **innovative Concept Designs** that help companies discover **better solutions** before **investing time** and resources in **bringing them to life**

concept-innovation-space._section: Homepage — 'Concept Innovation Space' (third section, below What Is KhanConcepts?) — 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).
concept-innovation-space.intro: Explore **innovative Concept Designs** across multiple **industries**, each focused on researching **real-world problems** and creating **better solutions**

how-we-bring-concepts-to-reality._section: Homepage — 'Concept to Solution Process' (fourth section, below Concept Innovation Space) — a vertical 4-step timeline. Each step's 'icon' must be one of: search, chat, document, handshake. 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).

steps.title: Explore Concepts
steps.description: Browse Concept Designs across multiple industries and discover the ones that align with your company's goals and interests.

steps.title: Concept Discussion
steps.description: Schedule a Concept Discussion to explore the Concept Design, discuss your company's goals, challenges, and requirements, and determine whether it's the right fit.

steps.title: Detailed Concept Design
steps.description: Once you decide to move forward, KhanConcepts creates a Detailed Concept Design tailored to your company's goals and requirements.

steps.title: Partnership
steps.description: Continue working with KhanConcepts as your Concept Design becomes a real-world solution.

featured-concepts._section: Homepage — 'Featured Concepts' (fifth section, below Concept Innovation Space — a rotating carousel). The heading is just 'Featured Concepts' itself, split white/gold. 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).

footer._section: Homepage — Footer (final section, at the very bottom of the page) — 'background.type' controls this section's background — set it to "none" for a plain black background, "image" to show background.image, or "video" to show background.video (a looping muted video file).

contact-page._section: Contact Page (the /contact route, opened from the bottom nav's 'Contact' button)
contact-page.headingWhite: Every Great Collaboration Starts With A
contact-page.paragraph1: Tell us about your company, your goals, the Concept Design you'd like to discuss, and any additional information that may help us prepare for the discussion.

whatsapp.label: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: Chat with us instantly on WhatsApp

form.detailsPlaceholder: Tell us about your company, your goals, the Concept Design you'd like to discuss, and anything you'd like us to know before the discussion.
form.successMessage: You will get the response from KhanConcepts Asap!

concepts-page._section: All Concepts page (/concepts) and every individual concept's detail page (/concepts/1, /concepts/2, ...) — the hero heading/tagline, the full Concept Space and Concept Type filter lists, and the master list of every concept card/detail page. 'spaces' and 'types' are the filter taxonomy — every concept's 'space'/'type' value must exactly match one entry from these two lists. Each entry in 'items' is one concept: 'status' must be either "new" or "improved", 'image' is the file path to its picture, 'overview' is 2-3 paragraphs shown on its detail page. 'video' is shown on the Concept Presentation section of the detail page — paste any regular YouTube or Vimeo link, or a direct hosted video file (.mp4 etc.); leave empty to show a placeholder. 'slideEmbed' is shown above the video — paste any regular Google Slides, Google Drive, or Canva share link (just the normal link you'd copy to share it, not a special embed code); leave empty to show a placeholder. 'problems' and 'opportunities' are legacy bullet lists kept for reference but no longer displayed on the detail page. 'showPresentation' controls whether the Concept Presentation section (slide deck) shows on that concept's detail page — defaults to true. 'showVideo' controls whether the separate Video section shows — defaults to false; set it to true once a concept has a real video to show.
concepts-page.tagline: Every great innovation starts with a **concept**.

items.title: NeoMetropolis AI City
items.description: An AI-orchestrated smart city framework that unifies infrastructure, mobility, energy, and governance into a single adaptive system built for sustainable urban growth.
overview: NeoMetropolis AI City is a civilization-scale urban operating system that coordinates transportation, energy, housing, and public services through one adaptive AI layer.

By modeling the city as a living system, the platform continuously rebalances resources — routing traffic, distributing power, and allocating services — in response to real-time demand instead of static planning.
The framework scales from a single smart district to an entire metropolitan region, giving city planners a foundation for resilient, sustainable growth.
problems: Legacy city infrastructure is siloed, with transport, energy, and services operating independently.
Urban planning reacts to congestion and resource strain after it happens, not before.
Rapid population growth is outpacing the capacity of existing city systems.
Sustainability targets are hard to hit without real-time visibility into resource use.
Emergency response is slowed by fragmented city data systems.
Citizens lack a single, unified way to interact with city services.
opportunities: Unify transportation, energy, and public services under one adaptive AI layer.
Reduce congestion and emissions through real-time resource routing.
Give planners predictive tools instead of reactive dashboards.
Improve emergency response with unified, real-time city data.
Create a scalable template usable across cities of any size.
Strengthen long-term sustainability and resilience planning.

items.title: BioLab Automation Suite
items.description: A robotics-driven laboratory automation suite that runs high-precision research workflows around the clock, freeing scientists from repetitive manual experimentation.
overview: BioLab Automation Suite coordinates robotic arms, sensors, and analysis software into one closed-loop research pipeline — from sample prep to data capture.
The system executes experiment protocols with sub-millimeter precision and repeats them continuously, removing the variability of manual handling.
Researchers design an experiment once; the suite runs it at scale, logs every result, and flags anomalies for human review.
problems: Manual lab work is slow, repetitive, and prone to human error.
Research throughput is capped by the number of hours scientists can physically work.
Experiment results vary depending on who performs the manual steps.
Valuable researcher time is spent on repetitive tasks instead of analysis.
Labs lack a consistent, automatic record of every experimental run.
Scaling research output usually just means hiring more lab staff.
opportunities: Run research protocols continuously, without staffing constraints.
Improve experiment consistency with robotic precision.
Free researchers to focus on analysis and discovery, not repetition.
Capture a complete, automatic log of every experimental run.
Scale research throughput without scaling headcount.
Flag anomalies automatically for faster human review.

items.title: Cognitive OS 2.0
items.description: An operating system built around a native AI layer that understands intent across every app, automating complex, multi-step tasks on a user's behalf.
overview: Cognitive OS 2.0 treats AI as a first-class layer of the operating system rather than a bolt-on assistant, giving it visibility across every installed application.
Users describe an outcome in plain language; the OS plans the steps, coordinates the relevant apps, and executes the task end-to-end.
The system is designed to hand off complex, multi-step work entirely — from research to booking to file organization — while keeping the user in control at every checkpoint.
problems: Today's AI assistants are siloed inside single apps and can't act across a device.
Users still perform most multi-step digital tasks manually, one app at a time.
Switching between apps to complete one task wastes time and attention.
Existing automation tools require technical setup most users can't do.
Privacy and control are often unclear when AI assistants act on a user's behalf.
Device settings and personalization rarely adapt automatically to how someone works.
opportunities: Let AI act across every app instead of being confined to one.
Turn multi-step digital chores into a single plain-language request.
Reduce the time users spend switching between apps manually.
Make automation accessible without any technical setup.
Keep the user in control with clear checkpoints and permissions.
Let the OS adapt to each person's workflow automatically.

items.title: EcoDrive Sustainable Mobility
items.description: A unified electric mobility platform that combines vehicle sharing, smart charging, and route optimization to cut urban transport emissions at scale.
overview: EcoDrive brings electric vehicle sharing, smart charging infrastructure, and AI-optimized routing into a single sustainable mobility platform.
The platform predicts demand across a city and repositions vehicles and charging capacity ahead of time, keeping the network efficient without idle assets.
Cities and fleet operators get one system for reducing emissions, cutting operating costs, and giving riders a seamless electric-first transport option.
problems: Urban transport remains a major source of carbon emissions.
EV charging infrastructure is fragmented and hard to plan around.
Fleet operators struggle to balance vehicle supply with real demand.
Riders lack a single, reliable electric alternative to private cars.
Idle vehicles and underused chargers waste capital and energy.
Cities lack real-time visibility into transport emissions data.
opportunities: Cut urban transport emissions with an electric-first shared fleet.
Predict demand and pre-position vehicles and charging capacity.
Give riders one seamless app for sustainable transport.
Lower fleet operating costs through smarter utilization.
Provide cities with real-time transport emissions data.
Build a template that scales across cities and regions.

items.title: Product Discovery Engine
items.description: An AI-powered discovery and comparison engine that finds the best products in any category and explains their real strengths, weaknesses, and value.
overview: Product Discovery Engine indexes products across every category and continuously scores them on features, strengths, weaknesses, and price relative to true competitors.
Instead of ranked ads, shoppers get a plain-language comparison and an AI voice or chat assistant that can walk them through any platform's instructions or demo on request.
The engine is built to support any product vertical — from electronics to services — with the same consistent, transparent comparison model.
problems: Shoppers can't easily compare a product's real strengths and weaknesses across brands.
Search results and marketplaces are dominated by paid placement, not merit.
Understanding how to actually use a new platform often has no clear guide.
Comparing similar products across categories takes hours of manual research.
Discounts and offers are scattered across platforms with no central view.
Most comparison tools stop at price and ignore feature depth.
opportunities: Give shoppers transparent, ranked comparisons based on merit, not ads.
Pair every product with an AI voice/chat guide and live demo.
Surface real strengths, weaknesses, and fit for a shopper's needs.
Centralize offers and discounts across every product category.
Cut research time from hours to a single conversation.
Apply the same trusted model across any product vertical.

items.title: NeuroLink Wearable Hub
items.description: A unified wearable ecosystem that connects watches, earbuds, and health sensors into one adaptive hub built around a single personal data account.
overview: NeuroLink Wearable Hub links a family of wearable devices — watch, earbuds, health sensors — into one coordinated system instead of separate, disconnected gadgets.
All device data flows into a single personal account, similar in spirit to an iCloud or Google account, giving users one place for backup, security, and cross-device continuity.
The hub is designed to extend to future wearables automatically, so new devices join the same ecosystem without new setup friction.
problems: Wearable devices from the same ecosystem often still feel disconnected.
Health and usage data is scattered across separate apps and accounts.
Setting up a new wearable device is repetitive and time-consuming.
Users have no single place to manage backup and security for all devices.
Battery and connectivity issues are handled inconsistently across devices.
Cross-device handoff (e.g. call to earbuds to watch) is often unreliable.
opportunities: Unify every wearable device under one connected hub.
Centralize personal data with one account for backup and security.
Make new device setup instant by joining the existing ecosystem.
Enable seamless handoff between watch, earbuds, and sensors.
Give users one dashboard for battery, health, and connectivity.
Build a foundation that scales to future wearable categories.

items.title: Quantum Cloud Framework
items.description: A cloud infrastructure layer built to run on quantum and fast-processor hardware, giving developers a migration path before quantum computing reaches the mainstream.
overview: Quantum Cloud Framework provides a cloud infrastructure layer designed from the ground up for quantum and next-generation processor hardware.
Developers get a familiar cloud interface while the framework handles the underlying complexity of quantum-safe execution and hybrid classical-quantum workloads.
The framework is built so existing cloud workloads can migrate incrementally, rather than requiring a disruptive rebuild once quantum hardware becomes mainstream.
problems: Most cloud infrastructure isn't designed for quantum or post-classical hardware.
Quantum-capable computers threaten to break current encryption standards.
Developers have no simple on-ramp to quantum-ready infrastructure today.
Migrating existing workloads to new hardware paradigms is disruptive.
Hybrid classical-quantum workloads are difficult to orchestrate manually.
Enterprises lack a clear timeline for when to start preparing.
opportunities: Give developers a familiar cloud interface over quantum-ready hardware.
Provide a quantum-safe migration path before disruption hits.
Support hybrid classical-quantum workloads out of the box.
Let enterprises migrate incrementally instead of rebuilding from scratch.
Position early adopters ahead of the quantum computing transition.
Turn quantum readiness into a competitive advantage rather than a risk.

items.title: RetailX Experience Model
items.description: A next-generation retail experience model that blends physical stores, AI-guided shopping, and try-on technology into one seamless customer journey.
overview: RetailX Experience Model reimagines the retail floor as an interactive system, combining AI product guidance, virtual try-on, and instant checkout into one journey.
Shoppers get AI assistance to compare products, see discounts, and try items virtually before committing to a purchase — in-store or online.
The model is designed to extend across formats, from flagship stores to pop-up retail, without rebuilding the experience each time.
problems: In-store and online retail experiences still feel disconnected.
Shoppers can't easily compare products or discounts while browsing in-store.
Trying products before buying is slow, especially across formats like apparel.
Retailers struggle to bring digital personalization into physical stores.
Checkout friction causes cart abandonment in both channels.
Expanding a retail concept to new formats usually means starting over.
opportunities: Unify in-store and online shopping into one seamless journey.
Bring AI product guidance and comparison onto the retail floor.
Let shoppers virtually try products before purchasing.
Personalize the physical store experience like an online one.
Reduce checkout friction with faster, AI-assisted flows.
Reuse one experience model across flagship, pop-up, and outlet formats.

items.title: Autonomous Transit Grid
items.description: An edge-computing transit network that lets autonomous vehicles make split-second routing decisions locally, without waiting on a distant central server.
overview: Autonomous Transit Grid pushes routing and safety decisions to edge compute nodes distributed across a transit network, instead of a single central cloud.
Vehicles and intersections communicate directly with nearby edge nodes, cutting the latency that autonomous systems can't afford when reacting to real-world conditions.
The network is built to scale city by city, adding edge capacity as autonomous vehicle density grows.
problems: Centralized cloud routing introduces latency autonomous vehicles can't afford.
Real-time safety decisions can't depend on a distant server responding in time.
Transit networks lack the local compute to react to sudden conditions.
Scaling autonomous fleets strains centralized infrastructure quickly.
A single point of failure can disrupt an entire transit network.
Cities have no standard framework for autonomous-ready infrastructure.
opportunities: Cut decision latency with distributed edge compute nodes.
Let vehicles and intersections react locally in real time.
Scale autonomous capacity city by city without central bottlenecks.
Remove single points of failure from transit infrastructure.
Give cities a standard framework for autonomous-ready networks.
Improve safety margins for autonomous vehicles at scale.

items.title: Synthetic Materials Lab
items.description: A computational materials research platform that simulates and screens novel synthetic materials before a single physical sample is produced.
overview: Synthetic Materials Lab combines simulation, machine learning, and lab automation to screen thousands of candidate material formulations computationally.
Only the most promising candidates move to physical synthesis, cutting the time and cost of traditional trial-and-error materials research.
The platform is designed for industries — from aerospace to energy storage — that depend on discovering better materials faster than competitors.
problems: Traditional materials research relies on slow, expensive trial and error.
Testing every candidate formulation physically is cost-prohibitive at scale.
Promising materials can take years to move from lab to production.
Research teams lack computational tools to screen candidates upfront.
Industries depending on new materials are bottlenecked by lab throughput.
Valuable formulations are often discovered by accident, not systematically.
opportunities: Screen thousands of material candidates computationally before synthesis.
Cut the time and cost of traditional trial-and-error research.
Move only the most promising candidates to physical testing.
Give research teams a systematic discovery process, not chance.
Accelerate materials breakthroughs for aerospace, energy, and beyond.
Build a reusable platform across many materials research programs.

items.title: Digital Civic Framework
items.description: A decentralized civic governance framework that lets citizens propose, deliberate, and ratify local policy with the precision of legal language.
overview: Digital Civic Framework gives citizens a direct, decentralized channel to propose and deliberate on local policy, removing the logistical bottlenecks of traditional governance.
Proposals are drafted in plain language, while an AI legislative layer simultaneously generates binding legal text visible to every participant in real time.
The framework is designed to scale from a single district to national governance, with every decision recorded transparently on a shared civic ledger.
problems: Citizens have few direct, meaningful channels to shape local policy.
Traditional legislative processes move too slowly for modern needs.
Legal language is inaccessible to most citizens who want to participate.
Governance systems lack transparent, tamper-proof record-keeping.
Digital identity and voting systems remain fragmented and insecure.
Trust in institutions erodes without visible, verifiable decision-making.
opportunities: Give citizens a direct channel to propose and ratify local policy.
Translate plain-language proposals into binding legal text instantly.
Record every civic decision on a transparent, shared ledger.
Rebuild institutional trust through visible, verifiable governance.
Scale participatory governance from a district to a nation.
Secure digital identity and voting within one civic framework.

items.title: ImmersiVerse Experience
items.description: A shared virtual-reality entertainment world where players, creators, and audiences meet, compete, and build experiences together in real time.
overview: ImmersiVerse Experience is a persistent virtual world combining social spaces, competitive play, and creator tools into one connected VR entertainment platform.
Players can meet, talk, and compete in real time, while creators build and monetize original experiences directly inside the same shared world.
The platform is designed around fair, skill-based competition rather than chance, with virtual economies that keep value inside the ecosystem.
problems: VR entertainment platforms are often isolated experiences, not shared worlds.
Creators have limited tools to build and monetize inside virtual spaces.
Competitive gaming is frequently undermined by pay-to-win mechanics.
Social presence in VR still feels disconnected from real interaction.
Virtual economies are fragmented across incompatible platforms.
Anti-cheat and fairness systems lag behind traditional gaming platforms.
opportunities: Unite social, competitive, and creator experiences in one VR world.
Give creators native tools to build and monetize original content.
Base competition on skill, with strong anti-cheat protections.
Make social presence in VR feel genuinely connected.
Keep a consistent virtual economy across the whole platform.
Set a new fairness and trust standard for VR entertainment.
